question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $92,000 on November 19?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: How to See Candle Closing Timer on the Chart 1. Open the
evidences.content: Image 1: Binance Square Login Sign Up DiscoverNewsNotificationProfileBookmarksChatsHistoryCreator CenterSettings Post Image 2 UR-NUMAN May 4, 2025 Follow Report Block User How to See Candle Closing Timer on the Chart 1. Open the Chart: Open the chart of the coin you want to analyze (e.g., $BTC /$USDT). 2. Select Timeframe: Choose your preferred timeframe from the top (like 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, etc.). 3. Click the Settings Icon: Look for the settings icon on the top or side of the chart and click it. 4. Find “Countdown to Bar Close”: In the settings menu, locate the option called “Countdown to bar close” (or similar wording) and enable it. 5. Save Settings: Save your settings. [...] 5. Save Settings: Save your settings. Now, your chart will show a timer on each candlestick — letting you know exactly when the current candle will close. So when I mention a 4-hour candle price, be patient and observe where the candle closes. Even $1 matters — don’t make decisions in a hurry. JazakAllah! If this post helped you, a like would be appreciated! #CryptoTips#TradingView#CandlestickTimer#PatienceInTrading Image 3 Image 4 Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. No advice. evidences.content: Bitcoin is trading essentially flat, hovering near $63,100 after a volatile session that saw an intraday high near $64,200 before sellers pushed back. The week's narrative has been driven by a weak June jobs report (57,000 vs. 113,000 forecast) that triggered a short squeeze and roughly $450M in short liquidations, but today's mood turned cautious after Strategy (MSTR) disclosed it sold 3,588 BTC (~$216M) to fund preferred stock dividends — its largest operational Bitcoin sale since 2022. Bernstein analysts noted Strategy has been 'a balancing force' limiting deeper selloffs, making the disclosure a sentiment shock. Meanwhile, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs snapped a 10-day outflow streak with $221M in inflows, whale accumulation of $16.7B over two weeks signals structural support near $60K, and [...] 0.14% Bitcoin is trading essentially flat near $64,000, holding above key support after a sharp intraday swing from $63,784 to $65,469. The primary narrative centers on a meaningful deceleration in spot ETF outflows — weekly redemptions fell roughly 87% from early June peaks — signaling the heaviest phase of institutional selling may be passing. Strategy added another 520 BTC for ~$35 mill...

evidences.title: A Beginner’s Guide to Binance TradingView Charts: Reading Like a Pro
evidences.content: What Is a TradingView Chart? A TradingView chart is a visual tool that shows the price movement of a cryptocurrency over time. Instead of just looking at numbers, charts let you see trends, patterns, and signals. Think of it as a map for price action it tells you where the market has been, and helps you guess where it might go. Choosing Timeframes On Binance, you can switch between different timeframes like: 1m (1 minute) – shows very short-term moves. 1h (1 hour) – useful for day trading. 1D (1 day) – perfect for spotting long-term trends. Image 4 Rule of thumb: Short timeframes = more noise, long timeframes = clearer trends. Candlesticks Explained The default view on Binance is candlestick charts. Each candle shows four things: Open – where price started. [...] Open – where price started. Close – where price ended. High – the highest point in that period. Low – the lowest point in that period. A green candle = price went up. A red candle = price went down. Together, candlesticks form patterns that help traders spot opportunities. Image 5 Basic Tools Every Beginner Should Know Binance’s TradingView gives you dozens of tools, but here are the essentials: Trendlines → Draw lines...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price History - Investopedia
evidences.content: By the summer of 2021, however, prices were down by 50%, closing at $30,838 on July 19. September saw another bull run, with BTC scraping $52,660, but a significant drawdown led it to close at $42,200 about two weeks later.1 On Nov. 8, 2021, Bitcoin reached another all-time high of $67,549. In mid-December 2021, the asset fell to a close of $46,709. The price started fluctuating more as uncertainty about inflation and the emergence of a new variant of COVID-19, Omicron, continued to spook investors.1 2 Between January and May 2022, Bitcoin's price continued to gradually decline, with closing prices only reaching $47,100 by the end of March before falling further to $29,101 on May 23. This was the first time since July 2021 that Bitcoin closed under $30,000.1 [...] ### Late 2024 On Nov. 7, 2024, Bitcoin's price hit another all-time high of $75,902 following Donald Trump's re-election, a 9% increase from the closing price on Nov. 5. On Nov. 10, 2024, Bitcoin reached another milestone, crossing the $80,000 threshold.1 On Nov. 11, it continued pushing boundaries, and two days later, it closed at $90,574.1 Market exuberance continued apace in the following weeks, pushing Bitcoin to r...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Surges: Dramatic 1.97% Five-Minute Rally on Binance USDT ...
evidences.content: ## Analyzing the Bitcoin Price Surge A 1.97% gain in five minutes represents a substantial move for an asset of Bitcoin’s market capitalization. For context, this equates to an approximate price increase of over $1,350 per BTC in that short period. Such intraday volatility is not uncommon for cryptocurrency markets, yet it demands a closer examination of underlying factors. Market data from Binance, one of the world’s largest digital asset exchanges, shows the surge occurred during a period of heightened trading volume on the BTC/USDT perpetual futures and spot markets. [...] ## Expert Insights on Rapid Price Movements Financial analysts specializing in digital assets emphasize a multi-faceted approach to interpreting sudden price jumps. Dr. Anya Sharma, a market microstructure researcher, notes, “Short-term volatility spikes are often noise within a longer-term trend. The critical task is distinguishing between organic market activity and potential market anomalies or manipulation. Data from on-chain analytics, such as exchange net flows and miner activity, provides essential context beyond the price chart.” [...] ### The Role of Market Liquidity and Structure Market structure...

evidences.title: 5-Minute Hack to Understand Binance Charts Like a Pro
evidences.content: Image 4 2. Current Price & 24h Performance At the top of the screen, Binance highlights the latest market price alongside: 24h High & Low → The highest and lowest prices reached in the past day. 24h Change (%) → Percentage increase or decrease within 24 hours. These figures quickly indicate the asset’s short-term performance. Image 5 3. Candlestick Chart – The Core of Market Analysis The candlestick chart provides the clearest view of price action. Each candlestick represents a chosen timeframe (1 minute, 1 hour, 1 day, etc.): Green candle → Closing price is higher than the opening price (bullish). Red candle → Closing price is lower than the opening price (bearish). Wicks (shadows) → Show the highest and lowest prices reached during that period. [...] Candlesticks reveal market sentiment and help traders spot patterns of momentum, support, and resistance. Image 6 4. Timeframes – Seeing the Bigger Picture Charts can be adjusted by timeframe: Short-term (1m, 5m, 15m) → Useful for day trading and quick scalps. evidences.title: A dive into liquidity demographics for crypto asset trading | S&P Global
evidences.content: Figure 17 shows the market depth on Binance for the asset pair EUR-USDT in euros for the 1% and 10% band (up and down) from the midpoint of the bid-ask price. Of note is the shallow market of less than €5 million for the 1% band and less than €8 million for the cumulative 10% band. This is not surprising as stablecoins have more utility on-chain than off-chain. By contrast, on Binance, USDT liquidity is much greater against other digital assets such as ETH and BTC than against fiat markets.Figure 18 shows a much higher market depth for BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT for the 1% band around the mid-price of USDT 15 million and USDT 30 million, respectively. [...] Figure 19 shows the average 1% market depth for selected markets (Binance) is lowest for EUR-USDC and highest for BTC-USDT. Crypto-USDT markets show a significantly higher market depth than crypto-fiat markets, which involve banks, fees, know your customer and other compliance measures. Crypto-USDT does not have any traditional banking constraints and is integrated seamlessly into trading strategies. External events or market news can affect market depth. During events of high volatility, such as the Silicon Valley Bank crisis in...

evidences.content: In this interview, Adam Morgan McCarthy covers: ✅ Liquidity flywheel explained: why depth + volume attract more flow — and reinforce reliability ✅ 2016–2017 market structure: few venues, clunky UX, limited pairs — and why Binance scaled fast ✅ Stablecoin pairs as a breakthrough: global access, smoother cross‑venue quoting, fewer liquidity gaps ✅ Depth metrics that matter: mid‑price depth at 1–2% bands, bids/asks summed — and spotting volume/depth mismatches ✅ Post‑FTX recovery: how liquidity “closed the gap” across venues and what improved ✅ October 2025 volatility: macro uncertainty + weekend liquidity patterns — and the rapid rebound ✅ Big venues, big debate: why dominant liquid venues aid price discovery and institutional execution [...] From the fragmented 2016–2017 exchange landscape to today’s globally quoted stablecoin pairs, Adam explains how structure changes don’t always show up in price — but they do show up in execution. evidences.title: Prediction of Cryptocurrency Prices through a Path Dependent ...
evidences.content: The cryptocurrency market behaves quite differently from the traditional stock market. It operates continuously around the clock and shows signs of extreme volatility. Notably, it lacks market makers and experiences low liquidity, factors that contribute to its increased volatility and susceptibility to sudden price jumps. For instance, the total market capitalization of Bitcoin was $1.2 billion in May 2013, which grew to $100 billion mark in October 2017 and briefly reached its highest market capitalization of $1.28 trillion mark in November 2021. This unique behavior has garnered significant interest in recent times, making cryptocurrency markets an intriguing area of study for researchers and investors. Several researchers have studied cryptocurrency price forecasting, employing [...] We use freely available OHLCV data from the popular cryptocurrency exchange binance.com, focusing on the BTCUSDT pair. Data for other pairs such as ETHUSDT and ADAUSDT can also be obtained for conducting similar simulations. Specifically, we used the Klines spot data for the BTCUSDT pair with the granularity of ‘1h’ from January 2020 to January 2023. The data provided by Binance was organized on...
